title: Implement React hooks for indexer queries
markdown:
## Goal

Create React hooks that wrap the `HttpIndexerClient`, providing declarative data fetching for indexed queries, search, and aggregation. These hooks handle loading states, error handling, caching, and refetching.

## Background

G1-A6-09 provides the `HttpIndexerClient` for remote indexer access. React apps need hooks that integrate with React's lifecycle (fetch on mount, refetch on dependency changes, cleanup on unmount).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `useIndexedQuery<T>(query: IndexQuery, options?)` hook:
  - Fetches on mount and when query changes (deep comparison)
  - Returns `{ data: IndexQueryResult<T> | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null; refetch: () => void }`
  - Options: `{ enabled?: boolean; refetchInterval?: number; client?: HttpIndexerClient }`
- [ ] `useIndexedSearch<T>(text: string, options?)` hook:
  - Debounces search text (300ms default, configurable)
  - Returns `{ results: IndexQueryResult<T> | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null }`
  - Options: `{ debounceMs?: number; fields?: string[]; limit?: number; enabled?: boolean }`
- [ ] `useAggregation(pipeline: AggregationStage[], options?)` hook:
  - Returns `{ data: AggregationResult | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null; refetch: () => void }`
- [ ] `useIndexerHealth()` hook:
  - Returns `{ health: IndexerHealthStatus | null; loading: boolean }`
  - Polls every 30 seconds (configurable)
- [ ] All hooks handle component unmount (abort in-flight requests)
- [ ] All hooks support an `enabled` option to conditionally skip fetching

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/` for existing hook patterns
2. Create each hook file:
   - Use `useState` for data/loading/error state
   - Use `useEffect` for fetch-on-mount and fetch-on-dependency-change
   - Use `useCallback` for refetch function
   - Use `useRef` for AbortController to cancel in-flight requests on unmount or re-render
   - For `useIndexedSearch`: use `useRef` + `setTimeout` for debounce
3. Create a shared utility:
   - `useIndexerClient()` hook or context that provides the `HttpIndexerClient` instance
   - Falls back to a default client created from environment/config
4. Example usage in comments for each hook

## Files to Create/Modify

- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useIndexedQuery.ts` -- new
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useIndexedSearch.ts` -- new
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useAggregation.ts` -- new
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useIndexerHealth.ts` -- new
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useIndexerClient.ts` -- new: shared client context/hook

## Testing

Manual testing in web rig:
```bash
cd apps/b3nd-web-rig && npm run dev
```

Create a test component that exercises each hook with the SDK inspector or test data.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-09 (HttpIndexerClient)
- **Blocks**: Nothing
